chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT HYDERABAD C.P.No.D-84 of 2022 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) 1. For orders on office objections. 2. For hearing of main case. 22.03.2023. Mr. Ayaz Muhammad Ghanghro, Advocate for petitioner alongwith petitioner. Mr. Muhammad Ismail Bhutto, Additional A.G alongwith Muhammad Paryal Morio DSP/SDPO Phuleli Hyderabad Inspector Najamuddin Nizamani on behalf of SSP Hyderabad and Inspector Javed Akhtar on behalf of DIGP Hyderabad. Mr. Bashir Ahmed Almani, Deputy Attorney General. Mr. Abdul Waheed Bijarani, Assistant P.G. = Today Reports are filed by SSP Hyderabad and SDPO Phuleli Hyderabad, which are taken on record. Fact of the matter is that detenue- Muddasar Ali Rajput is still missing. None of the Law Officers and SDPO Phuleli could give satisfactory reply about the whereabouts of the above named person. We are disappointed to see the contents of Progress Reports and the Composition of JIT (Joint Investigation Team) in which those representing different Agencies, their respective designation is not mentioned. On a specific query, it is stated that low ranking staff members attend JIT which itself proves that Government functionaries / Agencies are not serious in recovering and producing the detenue, who is a citizen of Pakistan and is studying in the University of Jamshoro. His father is also present and is clueless about the whereabouts of his son. The Government and Spy Agencies must realize their obligation towards citizens of Pakistan, particularly their life and liberty which are the guaranteed fundamental rights under the Constitution of Pakistan; and if an ordinary citizen is treated like this, then public confidence would be completely diminished in these Government functionaries, with far-reaching

His father is also present and is clueless about the whereabouts of his son. The Government and Spy Agencies must realize their obligation towards citizens of Pakistan, particularly their life and liberty which are the guaranteed fundamental rights under the Constitution of Pakistan; and if an ordinary citizen is treated like this, then public confidence would be completely diminished in these Government functionaries, with far-reaching adverse consequences. It is their obligation that their actions should speak louder than words; but here the Reports are contrary to what we are observing. On the next date, senior representative(s) of Respondents No.3, 4 and 5, Military Intelligence (MI), Federal Investigation Agency (FIA) and Inter-Services Intelligence (ISI) should be present in Court. A Copy of this Order be communicated to the learned DAG, AAG and APG for compliance. To be listed on 06.04.2023 at 11:00 a.m. JUDGE JUDGE Shahid
